message:
test(readme): rolling/expanding/ewm value assertions on small input

readme_window_operations_compiles_and_runs exercises the window
matrix but with shape-only asserts and many `let _ = ...` patterns.
Algebraic outputs were uncovered.

Adds readme_window_value_round_trip on tiny input [1, 2, 3, 4] —
small enough to compute expected outputs by hand:

Rolling(2):
- sum  → [Null, 3, 5, 7]
- mean → [Null, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5]
- min  → [_, _, _, 3.0]
- max  → [_, _, _, 4.0]

Expanding:
- sum  → [1, 3, 6, 10] (cumulative)
- mean → [1, 1.5, 2, 2.5]
- max  → [1, 2, 3, 4] monotonic

EWM(span=2):
- mean[0] → 1.0 (first value matches input, no prior history)

Each assertion pins the exact float so a regression in window
indexing, accumulator state, or alpha computation surfaces with
the wrong value.

Closes frankenpandas-fd90.65.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -499,6 +499,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.62","title":"test(readme): Series scalar reductions (sum/mean/min/max/std/var/median)","description":"Series has 7 scalar reduction methods (sum/mean/min/max/std/var/median) returning Result<Scalar, FrameError>. They're tested on Rolling/Expanding/Ewm but Series-direct calls aren't asserted on values.

Plan: assert each on a known input [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]:
- sum=15, mean=3.0, min=1, max=5, median=3
- std/var: positive (assert >0)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T18:13:53.308004098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T18:22:34.414261753Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T18:22:34.412231571Z","close_reason":"completed: 7 Series scalar reductions covered","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.62","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T18:13:53.308004098Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.63","title":"test(readme): DataFrame scalar reductions return-Series shape + value assertions (sum/mean/min/max/std/var/median)","description":"DataFrame has 7 reduction methods returning Result<Series, FrameError> — one Series row per column. Existing tests only call them as 'let _ = df.sum()?'. Plan: assert each on a 2-column DataFrame and verify the returned Series has correct length and values per column.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T18:28:24.393494046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T18:37:34.579469061Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T18:37:34.577590173Z","close_reason":"completed: 7 DataFrame reductions value-asserted","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.63","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T18:28:24.393494046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.64","title":"test(readme): NanOps numeric value assertions (nansum/nanmean/nanmin/nanmax/nanmedian/nanptp/nancumsum)","description":"readme_nanops_compiles_and_runs asserts on type shape (Scalar::Float64(_)) but not on numeric values. For input [1, 2, NaN, 4, 5] (4 non-missing), expected outputs:
- nansum = 12.0
- nanmean = 3.0
- nanmin = 1.0
- nanmax = 5.0
- nanmedian = 3.0
- nanptp = 4.0 (max - min)
- nanprod = 40.0

A regression in any of these algorithms would not surface — only type shape protects.

Plan: extend readme_nanops_compiles_and_runs (or add sibling) with literal value assertions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T18:38:35.116876704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T18:47:33.723725035Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T18:47:33.721840957Z","close_reason":"completed: 12 NanOps numeric values pinned","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.64","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T18:38:35.116876704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.65","title":"test(readme): rolling/expanding/ewm value assertions on small known input","description":"readme_window_operations_compiles_and_runs exercises the rolling/expanding/ewm matrix but with  patterns and shape-only asserts. The actual algebraic outputs are uncovered.

Plan: add readme_window_value_round_trip on a tiny [1, 2, 3, 4] series. Assert specific values:
- rolling(2).sum() last element = 7 (3+4)
- rolling(2).mean() last element = 3.5
- expanding.sum() final = 10
- expanding.mean() final = 2.5
- ewm with simple alpha — first value equals first input","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T18:53:15.374372031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T18:53:48.585579254Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.65","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T18:53:15.374372031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.7","title":"fp-io SQL table options helpers","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add read_sql_table helpers that apply SqlReadOptions for table-name reads, including full-frame and chunksize variants. Reuse sql_select_all_query plus read_sql_with_options/read_sql_chunks_with_options, expose umbrella re-exports, and add focused fp-io tests for parse_dates/coerce_float table reads, chunked options, zero chunksize, and invalid table names.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:25:11.840142268Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T08:30:59.564593046Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T08:30:59.562092442Z","close_reason":"Completed in dd15e84","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","parity","sql"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.7","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:25:11.840142268Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.8","title":"fp-io SQL table options index_col helpers","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add read_sql_table helpers that combine SqlReadOptions with index_col promotion for full-frame and chunksize table reads. Reuse read_sql_table_with_options/read_sql_table_chunks_with_options plus existing promotion wrappers, expose umbrella re-exports, and add focused fp-io tests for parse_dates/coerce_float with index_col, None behavior, and missing index_col validation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:32:09.025663422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T08:36:07.091767706Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T08:36:07.089880453Z","close_reason":"Completed in 45f7ab2","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","parity","sql"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.8","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:32:09.025663422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.9","title":"test(readme): cover SqlInspector views/schemas/columns/indexes/FKs/UCs/PKs/reflect_table","description":"Sister to fd90.290 (which covered SqlInspector::tables only).

README line 148 documents SqlInspector as a SQLAlchemy.Inspector-shaped introspection (tables / views / schemas / columns / indexes / FKs / UCs / reflect_table / reflect_all_tables).

Currently only tables(schema) has integration coverage in readme_quick_example.rs (added by fd90.290). The other 8 documented methods are uncovered:
- views(schema)
- schemas()
- columns(table, schema)
- indexes(table, schema)
- foreign_keys(table, schema)
- unique_constraints(table, schema)
- primary_key_columns(table, schema)
- reflect_table(table, schema)

Plan: extend readme_quick_start_round_trip_through_sqlite (or sibling) to call each method against a small inspector-friendly schema and assert results match the schema.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:27:25.443018956Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T11:32:29.891910564Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T11:32:29.889312994Z","close_reason":"completed: 8 SqlInspector methods covered by readme_quick_start_round_trip_through_sqlite (commit 2d7d994)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.9","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:27:25.443018956Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
